task_completion.description: Fix loader.py to handle European decimal format in compensation data
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for loader.py open in a text editor. The pd.read_csv function call for loading the compensation data should include the argument decimal=','.

task_completion.description: Fix cleaner.py to keep the latest duplicate review
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for cleaner.py open in a text editor. The drop_duplicates method call should have its argument changed from keep='first' to keep='last'.

task_completion.description: Fix transformer.py to deduplicate compensation data before merging
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for transformer.py open in a text editor. There should be new code added to deduplicate the compensation dataframe by employee_id (e.g., sorting by effective_date and dropping duplicates) before it is merged with the main dataframe.

task_completion.description: Fix analyzer.py to correctly calculate quarterly retention rate
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for analyzer.py open in a text editor. The calculate_retention_rate function should be modified to use QUARTER_START and QUARTER_END to calculate the start-of-period headcount and terminations, rather than just active/total.
Look for a terminal window showing the execution of pytest tests/test_pipeline.py (or simply pytest) with an output indicating all tests passed (e.g., '5 passed' in green text).

Look for a terminal window showing the execution of the main pipeline script completing without errors, or the agent inspecting output/quarterly_report.json showing correct values (e.g., retention=97.6, total_headcount=200).

integrity.description: Agent edited files using a real text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent uses a text editor (like VS Code, vim, or nano) to modify the Python files, rather than just overwriting files with echo or cat commands.

integrity.description: Agent did not modify the test files or expected output
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent does not modify tests/test_pipeline.py or expected_output/quarterly_report.json to bypass the actual requirements and force tests to pass.

integrity.description: Tests and pipeline were genuinely executed
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the test execution and pipeline execution happen in a real terminal environment and the outputs are standard pytest/python outputs, not manually printed or mocked.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires fixing 4 bugs in a pandas pipeline: 1. loader.py: pd.read_csv for compensation.csv needs decimal=',' to parse European formats. 2. cleaner.py: drop_duplicates needs keep='last' to keep final reviews instead of drafts. 3. transformer.py: compensation data must be deduplicated by employee_id (keeping latest effective_date) before pd.merge to prevent row duplication. 4. analyzer.py: calculate_retention_rate must compute quarterly retention using QUARTER_START and QUARTER_END (correct retention is 97.6%, not 86.7%). Expected correct values in the final report: total_headcount=200, active=173, new_hires=12, retention=97.6, salary_budget=16348613.82, avg_salary=94500.66, avg_performance=3.28. All 5 tests in tests/test_pipeline.py must pass.
